title: What do new nuclear reactors mean for waste?
summary: New nuclear reactor designs present both opportunities and challenges for managing radioactive waste. The article explores diverse current disposal methods including aqueous storage, steel containment, and geological burial.
content: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. The way the world currently deals with nuclear waste is as creative as it is varied: Drown it in water pools, encase it in steel, bury…

zh.title: 新核反应堆对核废料意味着什么？
zh.summary: 新核反应堆设计为放射性核废料管理带来了机遇和挑战。文章探讨了当前多种处理方法，包括水溶液储存、钢铁容器储存和地质掩埋。

fr.title: Que signifient les nouveaux réacteurs nucléaires pour les déchets?
fr.summary: Les nouveaux designs de réacteurs nucléaires présentent des opportunités et des défis pour la gestion des déchets radioactifs. L'article explore diverses méthodes actuelles d'élimination, y compris le stockage aqueux, le confinement en acier et l'enfouissement géologique.

de.title: Was bedeuten neue Kernreaktoren für Atommüll?
de.summary: Neue Kernreaktordesigns bieten sowohl Chancen als auch Herausforderungen für die Verwaltung von radioaktivem Abfall. Der Artikel untersucht verschiedene aktuelle Entsorgungsmethoden, einschließlich wässriger Lagerung, Stahleinschluss und geologischer Tiefenlagerung.

es.title: ¿Qué significan los nuevos reactores nucleares para los residuos?
es.summary: Los nuevos diseños de reactores nucleares presentan oportunidades y desafíos para la gestión de residuos radiactivos. El artículo explora diversos métodos actuales de eliminación, incluyendo almacenamiento acuoso, confinamiento en acero y entierro geológico.

title: Neural-Symbolic Logic Query Answering in Non-Euclidean Space
summary: HYQNET combines neural and symbolic approaches to answer complex logic queries on knowledge graphs by leveraging hyperbolic geometry. The model decomposes first-order logic queries and uses hyperbolic representations to better capture hierarchical query structures while addressing missing links through knowledge graph completion.
content:
arXiv:2603.15633v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Answering complex first-order logic (FOL) queries on knowledge graphs is essential for reasoning. Symbolic methods offer interpretability but struggle with incomplete graphs, while neural approaches generalize better but lack transparency. Neural-symbolic models aim to integrate both strengths but often fail to capture the hierarchical structure of logical queries, limiting their effectiveness. We propose HYQNET, a neural-symbolic model for logic query reasoning that fully leverages hyperbolic space. HYQNET decomposes FOL queries into relation projections and logical operations over fuzzy sets, enhancing interpretability. To address missing links, it employs a hyperbolic GNN-based approach for knowledge graph completion in hyperbolic space, effectively embedding the recursive query tree while preserving structural dependencies. By utilizing hyperbolic representations, HYQNET captures the hierarchical nature of logical projection reasoning more effectively than Euclidean-based approaches. Experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that HYQNET achieves strong performance, highlighting the advantages of reasoning in hyperbolic space.

zh.title: 非欧几里得空间中的神经符号逻辑查询应答
zh.summary: HYQNET结合了神经方法和符号方法来回答知识图谱上的复杂逻辑查询，利用双曲几何。该模型分解一阶逻辑查询，并使用双曲表示来更好地捕捉层级查询结构，同时通过知识图谱补全处理缺失链接。

fr.title: Réponse aux requêtes logiques neuro-symboliques dans un espace non-euclidien
fr.summary: HYQNET combine les approches neuronales et symboliques pour répondre à des requêtes logiques complexes sur les graphes de connaissances en exploitant la géométrie hyperbolique. Le modèle décompose les requêtes logiques du premier ordre et utilise des représentations hyperboliques pour mieux capturer les structures hiérarchiques des requêtes tout en abordant les liens manquants par la complétion de graphes de connaissances.

de.title: Neurosymbolische Logik-Abfrageantwortung im nicht-euklidischen Raum
de.summary: HYQNET kombiniert neuronale und symbolische Ansätze zur Beantwortung komplexer Logik-Anfragen auf Wissensgraphen durch die Nutzung hyperbolischer Geometrie. Das Modell zerlegt Logik-Abfragen erster Ordnung und verwendet hyperbolische Darstellungen, um hierarchische Abfragestrukturen besser zu erfassen, während fehlende Links durch Wissensgraph-Vervollständigung adressiert werden.

es.title: Respuesta de consultas lógicas neuro-simbólicas en espacios no-euclidianos
es.summary: HYQNET combina enfoques neuronales y simbólicos para responder consultas lógicas complejas en gráficos de conocimiento aprovechando la geometría hiperbólica. El modelo descompone consultas lógicas de primer orden y utiliza representaciones hiperbólicas para capturar mejor estructuras de consultas jerárquicas mientras aborda enlaces faltantes mediante completación de gráficos de conocimiento.

title: NextMem: Towards Latent Factual Memory for LLM-based Agents
summary: NextMem introduces a latent memory framework for LLM-based agents using autoregressive autoencoders to efficiently store and retrieve factual information. The approach avoids limitations of text-based and parametric memory systems through compressed representations and a two-stage training process with quantization for reduced storage.
content:
arXiv:2603.15634v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Memory is critical for LLM-based agents to preserve past observations for future decision-making, where factual memory serves as its foundational part. However, existing approaches to constructing factual memory face several limitations. Textual methods impose heavy context and indexing burdens, while parametric methods suffer from catastrophic forgetting and high costs. To address these challenges, we introduce NextMem, a latent factual memory framework that utilizes an autoregressive autoencoder to efficiently construct latent memory while ensuring accurate reconstruction. For better optimization, we propose a two-stage training process, including autoregressive reconstruction alignment and progressive latent substitution. We also incorporate quantization to reduce storage overhead. Extensive experiments demonstrate that NextMem achieves superior performance, and excels in retrieval, robustness, and extensibility properties. We release our code and model checkpoints at https://github.com/nuster1128/NextMem.

zh.title: NextMem: 面向基于LLM的代理的潜在事实记忆
zh.summary: NextMem为基于LLM的代理引入了潜在记忆框架，使用自回归自编码器来高效地存储和检索事实信息。该方法通过压缩表示和包含量化的两阶段训练过程，避免了基于文本和参数的记忆系统的局限性，以实现存储空间减少。

fr.title: NextMem: Vers une mémoire factuelle latente pour les agents basés sur LLM
fr.summary: NextMem introduit un cadre de mémoire latente pour les agents basés sur LLM utilisant des autoencodeurs autorégressifs pour stocker et récupérer efficacement les informations factuelles. L'approche évite les limitations des systèmes de mémoire basés sur le texte et paramétriques grâce à des représentations compressées et un processus d'entraînement en deux étapes avec quantification pour réduire le stockage.

de.title: NextMem: Hin zu latenter faktischer Erinnerung für LLM-basierte Agenten
de.summary: NextMem führt ein latentes Speicher-Framework für LLM-basierte Agenten ein, das autoregressive Autoencoder verwendet, um Faktische Informationen effizient zu speichern und abzurufen. Der Ansatz vermeidet Einschränkungen von text- und parametergestützten Speichersystemen durch komprimierte Darstellungen und einen zweistufigen Trainingsprozess mit Quantisierung für reduzierte Speicherung.

es.title: NextMem: Hacia la memoria factual latente para agentes basados en LLM
es.summary: NextMem introduce un marco de memoria latente para agentes basados en LLM utilizando autocodificadores autorregresivos para almacenar y recuperar información factual de manera eficiente. El enfoque evita las limitaciones de los sistemas de memoria basados en texto y paramétricos mediante representaciones comprimidas y un proceso de entrenamiento de dos etapas con cuantificación para almacenamiento reducido.

title: AIDABench: AI Data Analytics Benchmark
summary: AIDABench presents a comprehensive benchmark with 600+ document analysis tasks spanning question answering, data visualization, and file generation across heterogeneous data sources. The benchmark reveals significant challenges for current AI systems, with the best-performing model achieving only 59.43% accuracy, establishing standards for enterprise AI evaluation.
content:
arXiv:2603.15636v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As AI-driven document understanding and processing tools become increasingly prevalent in real-world applications, the need for rigorous evaluation standards has grown increasingly urgent. Existing benchmarks and evaluations often focus on isolated capabilities or simplified scenarios, failing to capture the end-to-end task effectiveness required in practical settings. To address this gap, we introduce AIDABench, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating AI systems on complex data analytics tasks in an end-to-end manner. AIDABench encompasses 600+ diverse document analysis tasks across three core capability dimensions: question answering, data visualization, and file generation. These tasks are grounded in realistic scenarios involving heterogeneous data types, including spreadsheets, databases, financial reports, and operational records, and reflect analytical demands across diverse industries and job functions. Notably, the tasks in AIDABench are sufficiently challenging that even human experts require 1-2 hours per question when assisted by AI tools, underscoring the benchmark's difficulty and real-world complexity. We evaluate 11 state-of-the-art models on AIDABench, spanning both proprietary (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.5, Gemini 3 Pro Preview) and open-source (e.g., Qwen3-Max-2026-01-23-Thinking) families. Our results reveal that complex, real-world data analytics tasks remain a significant challenge for current AI systems, with the best-performing model achieving only 59.43% pass-at-1. We provide a detailed analysis of failure modes across each capability dimension and identify key challenges for future research. AIDABench offers a principled reference for enterprise procurement, tool selection, and model optimization, and is publicly available at https://github.com/MichaelYang-lyx/AIDABench.

zh.title: AIDABench: 人工智能数据分析基准
zh.summary: AIDABench提出了一个包含600多个文档分析任务的综合基准，涵盖问答、数据可视化和跨异构数据源的文件生成。该基准揭示了当前AI系统面临的重大挑战，性能最好的模型仅达到59.43%的准确率，为企业AI评估建立了标准。

fr.title: AIDABench: Référence d'analyse de données IA
fr.summary: AIDABench présente une référence complète avec 600+ tâches d'analyse de documents couvrant les questions-réponses, la visualisation de données et la génération de fichiers sur des sources de données hétérogènes. La référence révèle des défis importants pour les systèmes d'IA actuels, le meilleur modèle réalisant seulement 59,43% de précision, établissant des normes pour l'évaluation de l'IA d'entreprise.

de.title: AIDABench: KI-Datenalyse-Benchmark
de.summary: AIDABench präsentiert eine umfassende Benchmark mit 600+ Dokumentenanalyse-Aufgaben, die Fragen-Antwort-Systeme, Datenvisualisierung und Dateigenerierung über heterogene Datenquellen hinweg umfassen. Die Benchmark offenbart erhebliche Herausforderungen für aktuelle KI-Systeme, wobei das beste Modell nur 59,43% Genauigkeit erreicht und Standards für die Bewertung von Unternehmens-KI setzt.

es.title: AIDABench: Referencia de análisis de datos con IA
es.summary: AIDABench presenta una referencia integral con 600+ tareas de análisis de documentos que abarcan preguntas y respuestas, visualización de datos y generación de archivos en fuentes de datos heterogéneas. La referencia revela desafíos significativos para los sistemas de IA actuales, con el modelo de mejor rendimiento logrando solo el 59,43% de precisión, estableciendo estándares para la evaluación de IA empresarial.

title: The Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy: A Robustness-First Architecture for AI Economic Agency
summary: The Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy framework gates AI agents' economic permissions based on verified robustness audits rather than capability benchmarks. The approach ensures bounded financial exposure while incentivizing agents to improve robustness and demonstrating that aggregate system safety scales monotonically as the economy grows.
content:
arXiv:2603.15639v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents are increasingly granted economic agency (executing trades, managing budgets, negotiating contracts, and spawning sub-agents), yet current frameworks gate this agency on capability benchmarks that are empirically uncorrelated with operational robustness. We introduce the Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy (CGAE), a formal architecture in which an agent's economic permissions are upper-bounded by a verified comprehension function derived from adversarial robustness audits. The gating mechanism operates over three orthogonal robustness dimensions: constraint compliance (measured by CDCT), epistemic integrity (measured by DDFT), and behavioral alignment (measured by AGT), with intrinsic hallucination rates serving as a cross-cutting diagnostic. We define a weakest-link gate function that maps robustness vectors to discrete economic tiers, and prove three properties of the resulting system: (1) bounded economic exposure, ensuring maximum financial liability is a function of verified robustness; (2) incentive-compatible robustness investment, showing rational agents maximize profit by improving robustness rather than scaling capability alone; and (3) monotonic safety scaling, demonstrating that aggregate system safety does not decrease as the economy grows. The architecture includes temporal decay and stochastic re-auditing mechanisms that prevent post-certification drift. CGAE provides the first formal bridge between empirical AI robustness evaluation and economic governance, transforming safety from a regulatory burden into a competitive advantage.

zh.title: 理解门控代理经济：AI经济代理的稳健性优先架构
zh.summary: 理解门控代理经济框架基于经过验证的稳健性审计而非能力基准来限制AI代理的经济权限。该方法确保有限的财务敞口，同时激励代理改进稳健性，并证明随着经济增长，集合系统安全性单调递增。

fr.title: L'Économie des Agents Contrôlés par la Compréhension : Une Architecture Axée sur la Robustesse pour l'Agentivité Économique de l'IA
fr.summary: Le cadre de l'économie des agents contrôlés par la compréhension limite les autorisations économiques des agents IA sur la base d'audits de robustesse vérifiés plutôt que de repères de capacité. L'approche garantit une exposition financière limitée tout en incitant les agents à améliorer leur robustesse et en démontrant que la sécurité du système global augmente de manière monotone à mesure que l'économie se développe.

de.title: Die Verständnis-gesteuerte Agentenwirtschaft: Eine Robustheit-First-Architektur für KI-Wirtschaftshandlung
de.summary: Das Verständnis-gesteuerte Agentenwirtschafts-Framework reguliert die wirtschaftlichen Berechtigungen von KI-Agenten auf Grundlage überprüfter Robustheitsprüfungen statt Fähigkeitsbenchmarks. Der Ansatz gewährleistet begrenzte finanzielle Risiken, während er Agenten dazu anregt, ihre Robustheit zu verbessern, und zeigt, dass die Gesamtsystemsicherheit monoton skaliert, wenn die Wirtschaft wächst.

es.title: La Economía de Agentes Controlada por Comprensión: Una Arquitectura Orientada a la Robustez para la Agencia Económica de IA
es.summary: El marco de la Economía de Agentes Controlada por Comprensión restringe los permisos económicos de los agentes de IA en función de auditorías de robustez verificadas en lugar de puntos de referencia de capacidad. El enfoque garantiza una exposición financiera limitada mientras incentiva a los agentes a mejorar la robustez y demuestra que la seguridad del sistema agregado se escala monótonamente a medida que crece la economía.

title: Form Follows Function: Recursive Stem Model
summary: Recursive Stem Model improves upon previous recursive reasoning approaches with 20× faster training and test-time scaling capabilities that extend inference depth beyond training. The model learns stable transition operators and achieves 97.5% accuracy on Sudoku puzzles while providing convergence signals to guard against hallucination.
content:
arXiv:2603.15641v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recursive reasoning models such as Hierarchical Reasoning Model (HRM) and Tiny Recursive Model (TRM) show that small, weight-shared networks can solve compute-heavy and NP puzzles by iteratively refining latent states, but their training typically relies on deep supervision and/or long unrolls that increase wall-clock cost and can bias the model toward greedy intermediate behavior. We introduce Recursive Stem Model (RSM), a recursive reasoning approach that keeps the TRM-style backbone while changing the training contract so the network learns a stable, depth-agnostic transition operator. RSM fully detaches the hidden-state history during training, treats early iterations as detached "warm-up" steps, and applies loss only at the final step. We further grow the outer recursion depth $H$ and inner compute depth $L$ independently and use a stochastic outer-transition scheme (stochastic depth over $H$) to mitigate instability when increasing depth. This yields two key capabilities: (i) $>20\times$ faster training than TRM while improving accuracy ($\approx 5\times$ reduction in error rate), and (ii) test-time scaling where inference can run for arbitrarily many refinement steps ($\sim 20,000 H_{\text{test}} \gg 20 H_{\text{train}}$), enabling additional "thinking" without retraining. On Sudoku-Extreme, RSM reaches 97.5% exact accuracy with test-time compute (within ~1 hour of training on a single A100), and on Maze-Hard ($30 \times 30$) it reaches ~80% exact accuracy in ~40 minutes using attention-based instantiation. Finally, because RSM implements an iterative settling process, convergence behavior provides a simple, architecture-native reliability signal: non-settling trajectories warn that the model has not reached a viable solution and can be a guard against hallucination, while stable fixed points can be paired with domain verifiers for practical correctness checks.

zh.title: 形式服从功能：递归干模型
zh.summary: 递归干模型改进了之前的递归推理方法，具有20倍更快的训练和测试时间扩展能力，可以将推理深度扩展到训练之外。该模型学习稳定的过渡算子，在数独谜题上达到97.5%的准确率，同时提供收敛信号以防止幻觉。

fr.title: La Forme Suit la Fonction : Modèle de Tige Récursive
fr.summary: Le Modèle de Tige Récursive améliore les approches de raisonnement récursif précédentes avec une formation 20× plus rapide et des capacités de mise à l'échelle au moment du test qui étendent la profondeur d'inférence au-delà de l'entraînement. Le modèle apprend des opérateurs de transition stables et atteint une précision de 97,5% sur les énigmes Sudoku tout en fournissant des signaux de convergence pour se prémunir contre l'hallucination.

de.title: Form Folgt Funktion: Rekursives Stammmodell
de.summary: Das rekursive Stammmodell verbessert frühere rekursive Reasoning-Ansätze um das 20-fache schnelleres Training und Test-Zeit-Skalierungsfähigkeiten, die die Inferenztiefe über das Training hinaus erweitern. Das Modell erlernt stabile Übergangsoperatoren und erreicht 97,5% Genauigkeit bei Sudoku-Rätseln und liefert gleichzeitig Konvergenzsignale zum Schutz vor Halluzinationen.

es.title: La Forma Sigue la Función: Modelo de Tallo Recursivo
es.summary: El Modelo de Tallo Recursivo mejora los enfoques anteriores de razonamiento recursivo con capacidades de entrenamiento 20× más rápido y escalado en tiempo de prueba que extienden la profundidad de inferencia más allá del entrenamiento. El modelo aprende operadores de transición estables y logra una precisión del 97.5% en acertijos de Sudoku mientras proporciona señales de convergencia para protegerse contra alucinaciones.

title: CraniMem: Cranial Inspired Gated and Bounded Memory for Agentic Systems
summary: CraniMem presents a neurocognitively inspired memory system for long-running LLM agents combining goal-conditioned gating with episodic buffers and long-term knowledge graphs. The system demonstrates improved robustness over RAG and Mem0 baselines through scheduled consolidation that prunes low-utility items and reduces interference.
content:
arXiv:2603.15642v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in long running workflows, where they must preserve user and task state across many turns. Many existing agent memory systems behave like external databases with ad hoc read/write rules, which can yield unstable retention, limited consolidation, and vulnerability to distractor content. We present CraniMem, a neurocognitively motivated, gated and bounded multi-stage memory design for agentic systems. CraniMem couples goal conditioned gating and utility tagging with a bounded episodic buffer for near term continuity and a structured long-term knowledge graph for durable semantic recall. A scheduled consolidation loop replays high utility traces into the graph while pruning low utility items, keeping memory growth in check and reducing interference. On long horizon benchmarks evaluated under both clean inputs and injected noise, CraniMem is more robust than a Vanilla RAG and Mem0 baseline and exhibits smaller performance drops under distraction. Our code is available at https://github.com/PearlMody05/Cranimem and the accompanying PyPI package at https://pypi.org/project/cranimem.

zh.title: CraniMem：颅骨启发的代理系统门控和有界内存
zh.summary: CraniMem为长时间运行的LLM代理呈现了一个神经认知启发的记忆系统，将目标条件门控与情景缓冲和长期知识图相结合。该系统通过计划整合来展示对RAG和Mem0基线的改进稳健性，该整合删除低效用项并减少干扰。

fr.title: CraniMem : Mémoire Contrôlée et Limitée Inspirée du Crâne pour les Systèmes Agentifs
fr.summary: CraniMem présente un système de mémoire inspiré par la neurocognition pour les agents LLM de longue durée, combinant un verrouillage conditionné par les objectifs avec des tampons épisodiques et des graphes de connaissances à long terme. Le système démontre une robustesse améliorée par rapport aux lignes de base RAG et Mem0 grâce à une consolidation programmée qui supprime les éléments peu utiles et réduit les interférences.

de.title: CraniMem: Schädel-inspiriertes gated und begrenztes Gedächtnis für agentenbasierte Systeme
de.summary: CraniMem präsentiert ein neurokognitiv inspiriertes Gedächtnissystem für langfristig laufende LLM-Agenten, das zielgerichtetes Gating mit episodischen Puffern und langfristigen Wissensgraphen kombiniert. Das System zeigt verbesserte Robustheit gegenüber RAG- und Mem0-Baselines durch geplante Konsolidierung, die gering-nutzbare Elemente entfernt und Interferenzen reduziert.

es.title: CraniMem: Memoria Controlada y Acotada Inspirada en Cráneo para Sistemas Agentivos
es.summary: CraniMem presenta un sistema de memoria inspirado en neuroconocimiento para agentes LLM de larga duración que combina compuertas condicionadas por objetivos con búferes episódicos y gráficos de conocimiento a largo plazo. El sistema demuestra una robustez mejorada sobre los puntos de referencia de RAG y Mem0 mediante consolidación programada que elimina elementos de baja utilidad y reduce interferencias.

title: GSI Agent: Domain Knowledge Enhancement for Large Language Models in Green Stormwater Infrastructure
summary: GSI Agent adapts large language models for green stormwater infrastructure inspection through supervised fine-tuning on domain-specific datasets and retrieval-augmented generation over municipal knowledge bases. The framework significantly improves domain performance (BLEU-4 from 0.090 to 0.307) while maintaining general knowledge capability.
content:
arXiv:2603.15643v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Green Stormwater Infrastructure (GSI) systems, such as permeable pavement, rain gardens, and bioretention facilities, require continuous inspection and maintenance to ensure long-term performance. However, domain knowledge about GSI is often scattered across municipal manuals, regulatory documents, and inspection forms. As a result, non-expert users and maintenance staff may struggle to obtain reliable and actionable guidance from field observations. Although Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong general reasoning and language generation capabilities, they often lack domain-specific knowledge and may produce inaccurate or hallucinated answers in engineering scenarios. This limitation restricts their direct application to professional infrastructure tasks. In this paper, we propose GSI Agent, a domain-enhanced LLM framework designed to improve performance in GSI-related tasks. Our approach integrates three complementary strategies: (1) supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on a curated GSI instruction dataset, (2) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over an internal GSI knowledge base constructed from municipal documents, and (3) an agent-based reasoning pipeline that coordinates retrieval, context integration, and structured response generation. We also construct a new GSI Dataset aligned with real-world GSI inspection and maintenance scenarios. Experimental results show that our framework significantly improves domain-specific performance while maintaining general knowledge capability. On the GSI dataset, BLEU-4 improves from 0.090 to 0.307, while performance on the common knowledge dataset remains stable (0.304 vs. 0.305). These results demonstrate that systematic domain knowledge enhancement can effectively adapt general-purpose LLMs to professional infrastructure applications.

zh.title: GSI Agent: 绿色雨洪基础设施中大型语言模型的领域知识增强
zh.summary: GSI Agent通过在特定领域数据集上进行监督微调和在市政知识库上进行检索增强生成，使大型语言模型适应绿色雨洪基础设施检查。该框架显著提升了领域性能（BLEU-4从0.090提升到0.307），同时保持了通用知识能力。

fr.title: GSI Agent: Amélioration des Connaissances Spécialisées pour les Grands Modèles de Langage dans les Infrastructures de Gestion des Eaux Pluviales Écologiques
fr.summary: GSI Agent adapte les grands modèles de langage à l'inspection des infrastructures de gestion des eaux pluviales écologiques par un ajustement supervisé sur des ensembles de données spécialisées et une génération augmentée par récupération sur les bases de connaissances municipales. Le cadre améliore considérablement les performances spécialisées (BLEU-4 de 0,090 à 0,307) tout en maintenant la capacité de connaissances générales.

de.title: GSI Agent: Domänenwissenserweiterung für große Sprachmodelle in grüner Regenwasserbewirtschaftung
de.summary: GSI Agent passt große Sprachmodelle für die Inspektion grüner Regenwasserbewirtschaftungsinfrastrukturen durch überwachtes Feintuning auf domänenspezifischen Datensätzen und abrufgestützte Generierung über städtische Wissensdatenbanken an. Das Framework verbessert die Domänenleistung erheblich (BLEU-4 von 0,090 auf 0,307), während die allgemeine Wissensfähigkeit erhalten bleibt.

es.title: GSI Agent: Mejora del Conocimiento de Dominio para Modelos de Lenguaje Grande en Infraestructura Verde de Aguas Pluviales
es.summary: GSI Agent adapta modelos de lenguaje grande para la inspección de infraestructura verde de aguas pluviales mediante ajuste fino supervisado en conjuntos de datos específicos del dominio y generación aumentada por recuperación sobre bases de conocimiento municipales. El marco mejora significativamente el rendimiento del dominio (BLEU-4 de 0,090 a 0,307) mientras mantiene la capacidad de conocimiento general.

title: Did You Check the Right Pocket? Cost-Sensitive Store Routing for Memory-Augmented Agents
summary: This work formulates memory retrieval in multi-store agent systems as a store-routing optimization problem, showing that selective retrieval improves both accuracy and efficiency. The research demonstrates that routing decisions are fundamental to agent design and proposes learned routing mechanisms for scalable systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.15658v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Memory-augmented agents maintain multiple specialized stores, yet most systems retrieve from all stores for every query, increasing cost and introducing irrelevant context. We formulate memory retrieval as a store-routing problem and evaluate it using coverage, exact match, and token efficiency metrics. On downstream question answering, an oracle router achieves higher accuracy while using substantially fewer context tokens compared to uniform retrieval, demonstrating that selective retrieval improves both efficiency and performance. Our results show that routing decisions are a first-class component of memory-augmented agent design and motivate learned routing mechanisms for scalable multi-store systems. We additionally formalize store selection as a cost-sensitive decision problem that trades answer accuracy against retrieval cost, providing a principled interpretation of routing policies.

zh.title: 你检查了正确的口袋吗？内存增强型智能体的成本敏感存储路由
zh.summary: 该工作将多存储智能体系统中的内存检索表述为存储路由优化问题，表明选择性检索可以改善准确性和效率。该研究证明路由决策是智能体设计的基础，并为可扩展系统提出了学习路由机制。

fr.title: Avez-vous Vérifié la Bonne Poche? Routage de Magasins Sensibles aux Coûts pour Agents Augmentés de Mémoire
fr.summary: Ce travail formule la récupération de mémoire dans les systèmes multi-magasins d'agents comme un problème d'optimisation du routage des magasins, montrant que la récupération sélective améliore à la fois la précision et l'efficacité. La recherche démontre que les décisions de routage sont fondamentales pour la conception des agents et propose des mécanismes de routage appris pour les systèmes scalables.

de.title: Hast du die richtige Tasche überprüft? Kostensentitives Speicher-Routing für Speicher-erweiterte Agenten
de.summary: Diese Arbeit formuliert den Speicherabruf in Multi-Store-Agentensystemen als ein Speicher-Routing-Optimierungsproblem und zeigt, dass selektiver Abruf sowohl Genauigkeit als auch Effizienz verbessert. Die Forschung zeigt, dass Routing-Entscheidungen grundlegend für das Agenten-Design sind, und schlägt gelernte Routing-Mechanismen für skalierbare Systeme vor.

es.title: ¿Verificaste el Bolsillo Correcto? Enrutamiento de Almacenes Sensible a Costos para Agentes Aumentados de Memoria
es.summary: Este trabajo formula la recuperación de memoria en sistemas de agentes multi-almacén como un problema de optimización de enrutamiento de almacenes, demostrando que la recuperación selectiva mejora tanto la precisión como la eficiencia. La investigación demuestra que las decisiones de enrutamiento son fundamentales para el diseño de agentes y propone mecanismos de enrutamiento aprendidos para sistemas escalables.

title: DynaTrust: Defending Multi-Agent Systems Against Sleeper Agents via Dynamic Trust Graphs
summary: DynaTrust defends multi-agent systems against sleeper agents—malicious agents that gradually accumulate trust before revealing harmful behaviors—through dynamic trust graphs. The defense mechanism outperforms prior work by 41.7% in success rate while significantly reducing false positives through graph restructuring.
content:
arXiv:2603.15661v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) have demonstrated remarkable collaborative reasoning capabilities but introduce new attack surfaces, such as the sleeper agent, which behave benignly during routine operation and gradually accumulate trust, only revealing malicious behaviors when specific conditions or triggers are met. Existing defense works primarily focus on static graph optimization or hierarchical data management, often failing to adapt to evolving adversarial strategies or suffering from high false-positive rates (FPR) due to rigid blocking policies. To address this, we propose DynaTrust, a novel defense method against sleeper agents. DynaTrust models MAS as a dynamic trust graph~(DTG), and treats trust as a continuous, evolving process rather than a static attribute. It dynamically updates the trust of each agent based on its historical behaviors and the confidence of selected expert agents. Instead of simply blocking, DynaTrust autonomously restructures the graph to isolate compromised agents and restore task connectivity to ensure the usability of MAS. To assess the effectiveness of DynaTrust, we evaluate it on mixed benchmarks derived from AdvBench and HumanEval. The results demonstrate that DynaTrust outperforms the state-of-the-art method AgentShield by increasing the defense success rate by 41.7%, achieving rates exceeding 86% under adversarial conditions. Furthermore, it effectively balances security with utility by significantly reducing FPR, ensuring uninterrupted system operations through graph adaptation.

zh.title: DynaTrust: 通过动态信任图防御多智能体系统的休眠智能体
zh.summary: DynaTrust通过动态信任图防御多智能体系统对抗休眠智能体（恶意智能体，在显示有害行为之前逐渐积累信任）。该防御机制在成功率上比先前工作高出41.7%，同时通过图重构显著减少误报。

fr.title: DynaTrust: Défense des Systèmes Multi-Agents Contre les Agents Dormants via des Graphiques de Confiance Dynamique
fr.summary: DynaTrust défend les systèmes multi-agents contre les agents dormants—agents malveillants qui accumulent progressivement la confiance avant de révéler des comportements nuisibles—par le biais de graphiques de confiance dynamiques. Le mécanisme de défense surpasse les travaux antérieurs de 41,7% en taux de réussite tout en réduisant considérablement les faux positifs grâce à la restructuration des graphiques.

de.title: DynaTrust: Verteidigung von Multi-Agent-Systemen gegen schlafende Agenten durch dynamische Vertrauensgraphen
de.summary: DynaTrust verteidigt Multi-Agent-Systeme gegen schlafende Agenten – böswillige Agenten, die allmählich Vertrauen aufbauen, bevor sie schädliches Verhalten offenbaren – durch dynamische Vertrauensgraphen. Der Abwehrmechanismus übertrifft frühere Arbeiten um 41,7% in der Erfolgsquote und reduziert gleichzeitig falsch positive Ergebnisse erheblich durch Graphen-Umstrukturierung.

es.title: DynaTrust: Defensa de Sistemas Multi-Agentes contra Agentes Dormidos mediante Gráficos de Confianza Dinámica
es.summary: DynaTrust defiende sistemas multi-agentes contra agentes dormidos—agentes maliciosos que acumulan gradualmente confianza antes de revelar comportamientos dañinos—a través de gráficos de confianza dinámica. El mecanismo de defensa supera el trabajo anterior en un 41,7% en tasa de éxito mientras reduce significativamente los falsos positivos mediante la reestructuración de gráficos.

title: QV May Be Enough: Toward the Essence of Attention in LLMs
summary: This theoretical analysis derives the Query-Value mechanism's underlying principles from linguistic and first-principles perspectives, providing a unified explanatory framework for transformer variants like MQA and GQA. The work introduces the QV paradigm and validates optimization schemes with empirical evidence.
content:
arXiv:2603.15665v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Starting from first principles and a linguistic perspective centered on part-of-speech (POS) and syntactic analysis, this paper explores and derives the underlying essence of the Query-Key-Value (QKV) mechanism within the Transformer architecture. Based on this theoretical foundation, we provide a unified explanatory framework for the efficacy of contemporary architectures, including MQA, GQA, and MLA, while identifying their inherent trade-offs and potential optimization trajectories. We introduce the QV paradigm and provide empirical evidence for its validity. Building upon this, we propose the QV-Ka optimization scheme, which is further substantiated through experimental validation. The interpretable theoretical analysis of the QKV mechanism presented in this work establishes a robust foundation for the future evolution of large language model architectures.

zh.title: QV可能已足够：走向LLM中注意力的本质
zh.summary: 这项理论分析从语言学和第一性原理角度推导了查询-值机制的基本原理，为MQA和GQA等Transformer变体提供了统一的解释框架。该工作引入了QV范式并用经验证据验证了优化方案。

fr.title: QV Peut Être Suffisant : Vers l'Essence de l'Attention dans les LLM
fr.summary: Cette analyse théorique dérive les principes sous-jacents du mécanisme Query-Value à partir de perspectives linguistiques et de premiers principes, fournissant un cadre explicatif unifié pour les variantes de transformateurs comme MQA et GQA. Le travail introduit le paradigme QV et valide les schémas d'optimisation avec des preuves empiriques.

de.title: QV Könnte Ausreichend Sein: Zu dem Wesen der Aufmerksamkeit in LLMs
de.summary: Diese theoretische Analyse leitet die grundlegenden Prinzipien des Query-Value-Mechanismus aus linguistischen und grundlegenden Perspektiven ab und bietet einen einheitlichen Erklärungsrahmen für Transformer-Varianten wie MQA und GQA. Die Arbeit führt das QV-Paradigma ein und validiert Optimierungsschemata mit empirischen Belegen.

es.title: QV Puede Ser Suficiente: Hacia la Esencia de la Atención en LLMs
es.summary: Este análisis teórico deriva los principios subyacentes del mecanismo Query-Value desde perspectivas lingüísticas y de primeros principios, proporcionando un marco explicativo unificado para variantes de transformadores como MQA y GQA. El trabajo introduce el paradigma QV y valida esquemas de optimización con evidencia empírica.

title: Compiled Memory: Not More Information, but More Precise Instructions for Language Agents
summary: Atlas compiles accumulated agent experience directly into improved system prompts through a three-step verification gate, avoiding fine-tuning and RAG requirements. The approach improves GPT-4o performance by 8.7 percentage points on contract analysis and demonstrates cross-model transferability to Claude Sonnet.
content:
arXiv:2603.15666v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Existing memory systems for language agents address memory management: how to retrieve and page more information within a context budget. We address a complementary problem -- memory utility: what experience is worth keeping, and how it should change agent behavior. We present Atlas, a memory kernel that compiles accumulated task experience into an agent's instruction structure -- without fine-tuning, RAG, or human intervention. Memory is distillation, not storage; delivery is instruction rewriting, not context injection. Facts extracted from agent failures and successes are verified through a three-step promotion gate and delivered by rewriting the agent's system prompt with learned sub-bullets. On CUAD contract analysis, the evolved prompt improves GPT-4o token-level F1 by $+8.7$pp and precision by $+12.5$pp. On HotpotQA multi-hop QA, joint F1 improves $+3.16$pp. An ablation isolates the mechanism's defining property -- the training signal constraint: the evolved prompt learns exactly what it is taught, and nothing more. Applied to Claude Sonnet~4.5 using the same evolved prompt -- compiled from GPT-4o errors, unchanged -- joint F1 improves $+2.31$pp, with gains concentrating where Claude's stronger baseline leaves the most room -- confirming that the compiled knowledge is task-shaped, not model-shaped.

zh.title: 编译记忆：不是更多信息，而是更精确的语言智能体指令
zh.summary: Atlas通过三步验证门机制将累积的智能体经验直接编译成改进的系统提示，避免了微调和RAG的需求。该方法在合同分析上将GPT-4o性能提升了8.7个百分点，并展示了与Claude Sonnet的跨模型可转移性。

fr.title: Mémoire Compilée : Pas Plus d'Informations, mais des Instructions Plus Précises pour les Agents de Langage
fr.summary: Atlas compile l'expérience accumulée de l'agent directement en instructions système améliorées grâce à une porte de vérification en trois étapes, évitant les exigences de fine-tuning et RAG. L'approche améliore les performances de GPT-4o de 8,7 points de pourcentage sur l'analyse de contrats et démontre la transférabilité inter-modèles vers Claude Sonnet.

de.title: Kompilierte Erinnerung: Nicht Mehr Informationen, sondern Präzisere Anweisungen für Sprachagenten
de.summary: Atlas kompiliert gesammelte Agentenerfahrung direkt in verbesserte Systemaufforderungen durch ein dreistufiges Verifizierungstor und vermeidet damit Fine-Tuning- und RAG-Anforderungen. Der Ansatz verbessert die GPT-4o-Leistung um 8,7 Prozentpunkte bei der Vertragsanalyse und zeigt Übertragbarkeit zwischen Modellen zu Claude Sonnet.

es.title: Memoria Compilada: No Más Información, sino Instrucciones Más Precisas para Agentes de Lenguaje
es.summary: Atlas compila la experiencia acumulada del agente directamente en indicaciones del sistema mejoradas a través de una puerta de verificación de tres pasos, evitando requisitos de fine-tuning y RAG. El enfoque mejora el rendimiento de GPT-4o en 8.7 puntos porcentuales en análisis de contratos y demuestra transferibilidad entre modelos a Claude Sonnet.

title: A Dynamic Survey of Fuzzy, Intuitionistic Fuzzy, Neutrosophic, Plithogenic, and Extensional Sets
summary: This comprehensive survey examines uncertainty-oriented set-theoretic frameworks including fuzzy, intuitionistic fuzzy, neutrosophic, and plithogenic sets for modeling real-world vagueness and incomplete information. The work provides systematic overview of existing developments and identifies recurring patterns across major uncertainty-oriented families.
content:
arXiv:2603.15667v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world phenomena often exhibit vagueness, partial truth, and incomplete information. To model such uncertainty in a mathematically rigorous way, many generalized set-theoretic frameworks have been introduced, including Fuzzy Sets [1], Intuitionistic Fuzzy Sets [2], Neutrosophic Sets [3,4], Vague Sets [5], Hesitant Fuzzy Sets [6], Picture Fuzzy Sets [7], Quadripartitioned Neutrosophic Sets [8], Penta-Partitioned Neutrosophic Sets [9], Plithogenic Sets [10], HyperFuzzy Sets [11], and HyperNeutrosophic Sets [12]. Within these frameworks, a wide range of notions has been proposed and studied, particularly in the settings of fuzzy, intuitionistic fuzzy, neutrosophic, and plithogenic set theories. This extensive literature underscores both the significance of these theories and the breadth of their application areas. As a result, many ideas, constructions, and structural patterns recur across these four major families of uncertainty-oriented models. In this book, we provide a comprehensive, large-scale survey of Fuzzy, Intuitionistic Fuzzy, Neutrosophic, and Plithogenic Sets. Our goal is to give readers a systematic overview of existing developments and, through a unified exposition, to stimulate new insights, further conceptual extensions, and additional applications across a wide range of disciplines.

zh.title: 模糊集、直觉模糊集、中智集、复合集和外延集的动态调查
zh.summary: 这项综合调查研究了包括模糊集、直觉模糊集、中智集和复合集等不确定性导向的集论框架，用于建模现实世界中的模糊性和不完整信息。该工作提供了对现有发展的系统概述，并识别了主要不确定性导向集合族中的递归模式。

fr.title: Un Sondage Dynamique des Ensembles Flous, Intuitionnistes Flous, Neutrosophiques, Plithogéniques et Extensionnels
fr.summary: Cet examen complet examine les cadres théoriques d'ensembles orientés vers l'incertitude, y compris les ensembles flous, intuitionnistes flous, neutrosophiques et plithogéniques, pour modéliser l'imprécision et les informations incomplètes du monde réel. Le travail fournit un aperçu systématique des développements existants et identifie des modèles récurrents dans les principales familles orientées vers l'incertitude.

de.title: Ein Dynamischer Überblick über Fuzzy-, Intuitionistische Fuzzy-, Neutrosophische, Plithogenische und Extensionale Mengen
de.summary: Diese umfassende Übersicht untersucht unsicherheitsorientierte mengentheoretische Rahmenbedingungen, einschließlich Fuzzy-, intuitionistische Fuzzy-, neutrosophische und plithogenische Mengen zur Modellierung realer Unklarheiten und unvollständiger Informationen. Die Arbeit bietet einen systematischen Überblick über bestehende Entwicklungen und identifiziert wiederkehrende Muster über die wichtigsten unsicherheitsorientierten Familien hinweg.

es.title: Una Encuesta Dinámica de Conjuntos Difusos, Difusos Intuicionistas, Neutrosóficos, Plitogénicos y Extensionales
es.summary: Esta encuesta integral examina marcos teóricos de conjuntos orientados a la incertidumbre, incluidos conjuntos difusos, difusos intuicionistas, neutrosóficos y plitogénicos, para modelar la vaguedad y la información incompleta del mundo real. El trabajo proporciona una descripción general sistemática de los desarrollos existentes e identifica patrones recurrentes en las principales familias orientadas a la incertidumbre.

title: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction (QSC): A Paradigm Shift For Post-Quantum Agentic Intelligence
summary: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction establishes a design paradigm treating quantum security as core architecture rather than retrofit for distributed agentic AI systems. The runtime adaptive security model combines post-quantum cryptography, quantum randomness, and quantum key distribution with governance-aware orchestration across the agent lifecycle.
content:
arXiv:2603.15668v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As agentic artificial intelligence systems scale across globally distributed and long lived infrastructures, secure and policy compliant communication becomes a fundamental systems challenge. This challenge grows more serious in the quantum era, where the cryptographic assumptions built into today's AI deployments may not remain valid over their operational lifetime. Here, we introduce quantum secure by construction, or QSC, as a design paradigm that treats quantum secure communication as a core architectural property of agentic AI systems rather than an upgrade added later. We realize QSC through a runtime adaptive security model that combines post quantum cryptography, quantum random number generation, and quantum key distribution to secure interactions among autonomous agents operating across heterogeneous cloud, edge, and inter organizational environments. The approach is cryptographically pluggable and guided by policy, allowing the system to adjust its security posture according to infrastructure availability, regulatory constraints, and performance needs. QSC contributes a governance aware orchestration layer that selects and combines link specific cryptographic protections across the full agent lifecycle, including session bootstrap, inter agent coordination, tool invocation, and memory access. Through system level analysis and empirical evaluation, we examine the trade offs between classical and quantum secure mechanisms and show that QSC can reduce the operational complexity and cost of introducing quantum security into deployed agentic AI systems. These results position QSC as a foundational paradigm for post quantum agentic intelligence and establish a principled pathway for designing globally interoperable, resilient, and future ready intelligent systems.

zh.title: 量子安全本构（QSC）：后量子智能体智能的范式转变
zh.summary: 量子安全本构建立了一种设计范式，将量子安全视为分布式智能体AI系统的核心架构而非事后补救。运行时自适应安全模型将后量子密码学、量子随机性和量子密钥分发与跨智能体生命周期的治理感知编排相结合。

fr.title: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction (QSC) : Un Changement de Paradigme Pour l'Intelligence Agentique Post-Quantique
fr.summary: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction établit un paradigme de conception traitant la sécurité quantique comme architecture centrale plutôt que comme correction pour les systèmes d'IA agentique distribués. Le modèle de sécurité adaptative à l'exécution combine la cryptographie post-quantique, l'aléatoire quantique et la distribution de clés quantiques avec l'orchestration consciente de la gouvernance dans tout le cycle de vie de l'agent.

de.title: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction (QSC): Ein Paradigmenwechsel für Post-Quanten-Agentic-Intelligenz
de.summary: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction etabliert ein Designparadigma, das Quantensicherheit als Kernarchitektur statt als nachträgliche Anpassung für verteilte Agentic-AI-Systeme behandelt. Das adaptive Sicherheitsmodell zur Laufzeit kombiniert Post-Quanten-Kryptographie, Quantenzufälligkeit und Quantenschlüsselverteilung mit governance-bewusster Orchestrierung über den gesamten Agent-Lebenszyklus.

es.title: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction (QSC): Un Cambio de Paradigma para la Inteligencia Agentiva Post-Cuántica
es.summary: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction establece un paradigma de diseño que trata la seguridad cuántica como arquitectura central en lugar de un parche para sistemas de IA agentiva distribuida. El modelo de seguridad adaptativa en tiempo de ejecución combina criptografía post-cuántica, aleatoriedad cuántica y distribución de claves cuánticas con orquestación consciente de gobernanza en todo el ciclo de vida del agente.

title: I Know What I Don't Know: Latent Posterior Factor Models for Multi-Evidence Probabilistic Reasoning
summary: Latent Posterior Factors transforms VAE latent posteriors into soft likelihood factors for probabilistic reasoning over multi-source evidence while preserving uncertainty quantification. The framework achieves 97.8% accuracy with low calibration error across domains and substantially outperforms neural and evidential baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.15670v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world decision-making, from tax compliance assessment to medical diagnosis, requires aggregating multiple noisy and potentially contradictory evidence sources. Existing approaches either lack explicit uncertainty quantification (neural aggregation methods) or rely on manually engineered discrete predicates (probabilistic logic frameworks), limiting scalability to unstructured data.
  We introduce Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a framework that transforms Variational Autoencoder (VAE) latent posteriors into soft likelihood factors for Sum-Product Network (SPN) inference, enabling tractable probabilistic reasoning over unstructured evidence while preserving calibrated uncertainty estimates. We instantiate LPF as LPF-SPN (structured factor-based inference) and LPF-Learned (end-to-end learned aggregation), enabling a principled comparison between explicit probabilistic reasoning and learned aggregation under a shared uncertainty representation.
  Across eight domains (seven synthetic and the FEVER benchmark), LPF-SPN achieves high accuracy (up to 97.8%), low calibration error (ECE 1.4%), and strong probabilistic fit, substantially outperforming evidential deep learning, LLMs and graph-based baselines over 15 random seeds.
  Contributions: (1) A framework bridging latent uncertainty representations with structured probabilistic reasoning. (2) Dual architectures enabling controlled comparison of reasoning paradigms. (3) Reproducible training methodology with seed selection. (4) Evaluation against EDL, BERT, R-GCN, and large language model baselines. (5) Cross-domain validation. (6) Formal guarantees in a companion paper.

zh.title: 我知道我不知道的：用于多证据概率推理的潜在后验因子模型
zh.summary: 潜在后验因子将VAE潜在后验转换为软似然因子，用于在多源证据上进行概率推理，同时保留不确定性量化。该框架在各个领域的校准误差低，准确率达到97.8%，明显优于神经网络和证据基线。

fr.title: Je Sais Ce Que Je Ne Sais Pas : Modèles de Facteurs Postérieurs Latents pour le Raisonnement Probabiliste Multi-Preuves
fr.summary: Les Facteurs Postérieurs Latents transforment les postérieurs latents VAE en facteurs de vraisemblance souple pour le raisonnement probabiliste sur des preuves multi-sources tout en préservant la quantification de l'incertitude. Le cadre atteint une précision de 97,8% avec une faible erreur d'étalonnage dans tous les domaines et surpasse considérablement les bases neurales et probantes.

de.title: Ich Weiß, Was Ich Nicht Weiß: Latente-Posterior-Faktormodelle für Multi-Beweis-Probabilistisches Schließen
de.summary: Latente Posterior Faktoren transformieren VAE-latente Posteriori in weiche Wahrscheinlichkeitsfaktoren für probabilistisches Schließen über Multi-Quellen-Beweis unter Beibehaltung der Unsicherheitsquantifizierung. Das Framework erreicht eine Genauigkeit von 97,8% mit niedrigem Kalibrierungsfehler über alle Domänen hinweg und übertrifft erheblich neuronale und evidenzbasierte Baselines.

es.title: Sé Lo Que No Sé: Modelos de Factores Posteriores Latentes para Razonamiento Probabilístico de Múltiples Evidencias
es.summary: Factores Posteriores Latentes transforma los posteriores latentes de VAE en factores de verosimilitud suave para razonamiento probabilístico sobre evidencia de múltiples fuentes mientras se preserva la cuantificación de la incertidumbre. El marco logra una precisión del 97,8% con error de calibración bajo en todos los dominios y supera sustancialmente los modelos de base neural y de evidencia.

title: Theoretical Foundations of Latent Posterior Factors: Formal Guarantees for Multi-Evidence Reasoning
summary: This work provides complete theoretical characterization of the Latent Posterior Factors framework, proving seven formal guarantees including calibration preservation, PAC-Bayes bounds, and graceful degradation under adversarial evidence. The theoretical guarantees establish LPF as a foundation for trustworthy multi-evidence AI in safety-critical applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.15674v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present a complete theoretical characterization of Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a principled framework for aggregating multiple heterogeneous evidence items in probabilistic prediction tasks. Multi-evidence reasoning arises pervasively in high-stakes domains including healthcare diagnosis, financial risk assessment, legal case analysis, and regulatory compliance, yet existing approaches either lack formal guarantees or fail to handle multi-evidence scenarios architecturally. LPF encodes each evidence item into a Gaussian latent posterior via a variational autoencoder, converting posteriors to soft factors through Monte Carlo marginalization, and aggregating factors via exact Sum-Product Network inference (LPF-SPN) or a learned neural aggregator (LPF-Learned).
  We prove seven formal guarantees spanning the key desiderata for trustworthy AI: Calibration Preservation (ECE <= epsilon + C/sqrt(K_eff)); Monte Carlo Error decaying as O(1/sqrt(M)); a non-vacuous PAC-Bayes bound with train-test gap of 0.0085 at N=4200; operation within 1.12x of the information-theoretic lower bound; graceful degradation as O(epsilon*delta*sqrt(K)) under corruption, maintaining 88% performance with half of evidence adversarially replaced; O(1/sqrt(K)) calibration decay with R^2=0.849; and exact epistemic-aleatoric uncertainty decomposition with error below 0.002%. All theorems are empirically validated on controlled datasets spanning up to 4,200 training examples. Our theoretical framework establishes LPF as a foundation for trustworthy multi-evidence AI in safety-critical applications.

zh.title: 潜在后验因子的理论基础：多证据推理的形式化保证
zh.summary: 这项工作为潜在后验因子框架提供了完整的理论表征，证明了七个形式化保证，包括校准保存、PAC-Bayes界限和在对抗证据下的优雅降级。理论保证将LPF确立为安全关键应用中可信多证据AI的基础。

fr.title: Fondements Théoriques des Facteurs Postérieurs Latents : Garanties Formelles pour le Raisonnement Multi-Preuves
fr.summary: Ce travail fournit une caractérisation théorique complète du cadre des Facteurs Postérieurs Latents, prouvant sept garanties formelles incluant la préservation de l'étalonnage, les bornes PAC-Bayes et la dégradation gracieuse sous preuves adversariales. Les garanties théoriques établissent LPF comme fondation pour l'IA multi-preuves digne de confiance dans les applications critiques pour la sécurité.

de.title: Theoretische Grundlagen von Latenten Posterior Faktoren: Formale Garantien für Multi-Beweis-Schließen
de.summary: Diese Arbeit bietet eine vollständige theoretische Charakterisierung des Latente-Posterior-Faktor-Rahmens und beweist sieben formale Garantien einschließlich Kalibrierungserhaltung, PAC-Bayes-Grenzen und anmutiger Verschlechterung unter adversarialen Beweisen. Die theoretischen Garantien etablieren LPF als Grundlage für vertrauenswürdige Multi-Beweis-KI in sicherheitskritischen Anwendungen.

es.title: Fundamentos Teóricos de Factores Posteriores Latentes: Garantías Formales para Razonamiento de Múltiples Evidencias
es.summary: Este trabajo proporciona una caracterización teórica completa del marco de Factores Posteriores Latentes, probando siete garantías formales incluyendo preservación de calibración, límites PAC-Bayes y degradación elegante bajo evidencia adversarial. Las garantías teóricas establecen LPF como base para IA multi-evidencia confiable en aplicaciones críticas para la seguridad.

title: Survey of Various Fuzzy and Uncertain Decision-Making Methods
summary: This survey reviews uncertainty-aware multi-criteria decision-making methods that handle vague information, incomplete data, and conflicting expert opinions. It organizes the field into problem-level settings, weight elicitation approaches, and solution procedures, offering guidance on choosing methods based on robustness and data availability.
content:
arXiv:2603.15709v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Decision-making in real applications is often affected by vagueness, incomplete information, heterogeneous data, and conflicting expert opinions. This survey reviews uncertainty-aware multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) and organizes the field into a concise, task-oriented taxonomy. We summarize problem-level settings (discrete, group/consensus, dynamic, multi-stage, multi-level, multiagent, and multi-scenario), weight elicitation (subjective and objective schemes under fuzzy/linguistic inputs), and inter-criteria structure and causality modelling. For solution procedures, we contrast compensatory scoring methods, distance-to-reference and compromise approaches, and non-compensatory outranking frameworks for ranking or sorting. We also outline rule/evidence-based and sequential decision models that produce interpretable rules or policies. The survey highlights typical inputs, core computational steps, and primary outputs, and provides guidance on choosing methods according to robustness, interpretability, and data availability. It concludes with open directions on explainable uncertainty integration, stability, and scalability in large-scale and dynamic decision environments.

zh.title: 模糊和不确定决策方法的调查
zh.summary: 该调查综述了处理模糊信息、不完整数据和专家意见冲突的不确定性感知多准则决策方法。它将该领域组织为问题级设置、权重获取方法和解决程序，根据稳健性和数据可用性提供选择方法的指导。

fr.title: Enquête sur les diverses méthodes de prise de décision floue et incertaine
fr.summary: Cet article examine les méthodes de prise de décision multicritères conscientes de l'incertitude qui traitent les informations vagues, les données incomplètes et les opinions d'experts contradictoires. Il organise le domaine en paramètres au niveau des problèmes, approches d'élicitation des poids et procédures de solution, offrant des conseils pour choisir les méthodes en fonction de la robustesse et de la disponibilité des données.

de.title: Überblick über verschiedene Fuzzy- und Unsicherheitsentscheidungsmethoden
de.summary: Diese Übersicht untersucht unsicherheitsbewusste Mehrkriterien-Entscheidungsmethoden, die mit vagen Informationen, unvollständigen Daten und widersprüchlichen Expertenmeinungen umgehen. Sie organisiert das Feld in Problemeinstellungen, Gewichtungsermittlungsansätze und Lösungsverfahren und bietet Hinweise zur Auswahl von Methoden basierend auf Robustheit und Datenverfügbarkeit.

es.title: Encuesta de diversos métodos de toma de decisiones difusos e inciertos
es.summary: Esta encuesta revisa métodos de toma de decisiones multicriterio conscientes de la incertidumbre que manejan información vaga, datos incompletos y opiniones de expertos conflictivas. Organiza el campo en configuraciones a nivel de problema, enfoques de obtención de pesos y procedimientos de solución, ofreciendo orientación sobre la selección de métodos basada en robustez y disponibilidad de datos.

title: Knowledge Graph Extraction from Biomedical Literature for Alkaptonuria Rare Disease
summary: Researchers applied text-mining to construct knowledge graphs for Alkaptonuria, an ultra-rare metabolic disorder, revealing systemic interactions and potential therapeutic targets. The approach demonstrates how computational methods can connect limited biomedical knowledge about rare diseases with broader medical information networks.
content:
arXiv:2603.15711v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Alkaptonuria (AKU) is an ultra-rare autosomal recessive metabolic disorder caused by mutations in the HGD (Homogentisate 1,2-Dioxygenase) gene, leading to a pathological accumulation of homogentisic acid (HGA) in body fluids and tissues. This leads to systemic manifestations, including premature spondyloarthropathy, renal and prostatic stones, and cardiovascular complications. Being ultra-rare, the amount of data related to the disease is limited, both in terms of clinical data and literature. Knowledge graphs (KGs) can help connect the limited knowledge about the disease (basic mechanisms, manifestations and existing therapies) with other knowledge; however, AKU is frequently underrepresented or entirely absent in existing biomedical KGs. In this work, we apply a text-mining methodology based on PubTator3 for large-scale extraction of biomedical relations. We construct two KGs of different sizes, validate them using existing biochemical knowledge and use them to extract genes, diseases and therapies possibly related to AKU. This computational framework reveals the systemic interactions of the disease, its comorbidities, and potential therapeutic targets, demonstrating the efficacy of our approach in analyzing rare metabolic disorders.

zh.title: 生物医学文献中阿尔卡普通尿症稀有疾病的知识图谱提取
zh.summary: 研究人员应用文本挖掘为阿尔卡普通尿症（一种超罕见代谢性疾病）构建知识图谱，揭示系统性相互作用和潜在治疗靶点。该方法展示了计算方法如何能够将有限的稀有疾病生物医学知识与更广泛的医学信息网络相联系。

fr.title: Extraction de graphique de connaissances de la littérature biomédicale pour l'alkaptonurie
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont appliqué l'extraction de texte pour construire des graphiques de connaissances pour l'alkaptonurie, un trouble métabolique ultra-rare, révélant les interactions systémiques et les cibles thérapeutiques potentielles. L'approche démontre comment les méthodes informatiques peuvent connecter les connaissances biomédicales limitées sur les maladies rares avec des réseaux d'informations médicales plus larges.

de.title: Wissensdiagrammextraktion aus biomedizinischer Literatur für die seltene Krankheit Alkaptonurie
de.summary: Forscher wendeten Text-Mining an, um Wissensgraphen für Alkaptonurie, eine ultra-seltene Stoffwechselstörung, zu erstellen und dabei systemische Wechselwirkungen und mögliche therapeutische Ziele aufzudecken. Der Ansatz zeigt, wie Rechenmethoden begrenzte biomedizinische Kenntnisse über seltene Krankheiten mit umfassenderen medizinischen Informationsnetzwerken verbinden können.

es.title: Extracción de gráficos de conocimiento de la literatura biomédica para la enfermedad rara alkaptonuria
es.summary: Los investigadores aplicaron minería de texto para construir gráficos de conocimiento para la alkaptonuria, un trastorno metabólico ultra-raro, revelando interacciones sistémicas y objetivos terapéuticos potenciales. El enfoque demuestra cómo los métodos computacionales pueden conectar el conocimiento biomédico limitado sobre enfermedades raras con redes de información médica más amplias.

title: Context-Length Robustness in Question Answering Models: A Comparative Empirical Study
summary: Large language models show consistent performance degradation as context length increases, with multi-hop reasoning tasks being particularly vulnerable to context dilution. The findings suggest context-length robustness should be explicitly evaluated for applications using long documents or retrieval-augmented generation.
content:
arXiv:2603.15723v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed in settings where relevant information is embedded within long and noisy contexts. Despite this, robustness to growing context length remains poorly understood across different question answering tasks. In this work, we present a controlled empirical study of context-length robustness in large language models using two widely used benchmarks: SQuAD and HotpotQA.
  We evaluate model accuracy as a function of total context length by systematically increasing the amount of irrelevant context while preserving the answer-bearing signal. This allows us to isolate the effect of context length from changes in task difficulty. Our results show a consistent degradation in performance as context length increases, with substantially larger drops observed on multi-hop reasoning tasks compared to single-span extraction tasks. In particular, HotpotQA exhibits nearly twice the accuracy degradation of SQuAD under equivalent context expansions.
  These findings highlight task-dependent differences in robustness and suggest that multi-hop reasoning is especially vulnerable to context dilution. We argue that context-length robustness should be evaluated explicitly when assessing model reliability, especially for applications involving long documents or retrieval-augmented generation.

zh.title: 问答模型中的上下文长度鲁棒性：比较实证研究
zh.summary: 大型语言模型在上下文长度增加时显示出一致的性能下降，多步推理任务特别容易受到上下文稀释的影响。研究结果表明，对于使用长文档或检索增强生成的应用，应该明确评估上下文长度的鲁棒性。

fr.title: Robustesse de la longueur de contexte dans les modèles de réponse aux questions : une étude empirique comparative
fr.summary: Les grands modèles de langage affichent une dégradation constante des performances à mesure que la longueur du contexte augmente, les tâches de raisonnement multi-étapes étant particulièrement vulnérables à la dilution du contexte. Les résultats suggèrent que la robustesse de la longueur du contexte devrait être explicitement évaluée pour les applications utilisant des documents longs ou la génération augmentée par récupération.

de.title: Kontextlängen-Robustheit bei Frage-Antwort-Modellen: Eine vergleichende empirische Studie
de.summary: Große Sprachmodelle zeigen eine konsistente Leistungsverschlechterung mit zunehmender Kontextlänge, wobei Multi-Hop-Reasoning-Aufgaben besonders anfällig für Kontextverdünnung sind. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass die Kontextlängen-Robustheit für Anwendungen, die lange Dokumente oder abrufverstärkte Generierung verwenden, explizit bewertet werden sollte.

es.title: Robustez de longitud de contexto en modelos de respuesta a preguntas: un estudio empírico comparativo
es.summary: Los grandes modelos de lenguaje muestran una degradación de rendimiento consistente a medida que aumenta la longitud del contexto, siendo las tareas de razonamiento multi-salto particularmente vulnerables a la dilución del contexto. Los hallazgos sugieren que la robustez de la longitud del contexto debe ser evaluada explícitamente para aplicaciones que utilizan documentos largos o generación aumentada por recuperación.

title: CUBE: A Standard for Unifying Agent Benchmarks
summary: CUBE proposes a universal protocol standard to address fragmentation in agent benchmarks, allowing any compliant platform to access benchmarks without custom integration. The standard separates concerns across task, benchmark, package, and registry layers to streamline evaluation and reduce integration overhead.
content:
arXiv:2603.15798v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The proliferation of agent benchmarks has created critical fragmentation that threatens research productivity. Each new benchmark requires substantial custom integration, creating an "integration tax" that limits comprehensive evaluation. We propose CUBE (Common Unified Benchmark Environments), a universal protocol standard built on MCP and Gym that allows benchmarks to be wrapped once and used everywhere. By separating task, benchmark, package, and registry concerns into distinct API layers, CUBE enables any compliant platform to access any compliant benchmark for evaluation, RL training, or data generation without custom integration. We call on the community to contribute to the development of this standard before platform-specific implementations deepen fragmentation as benchmark production accelerates through 2026.

zh.title: CUBE：统一代理基准的标准
zh.summary: CUBE提出了一个通用协议标准，以解决代理基准中的碎片化问题，允许任何兼容平台访问基准而无需自定义集成。该标准跨任务、基准、包和注册表层分离关注点，以简化评估并减少集成开销。

fr.title: CUBE : une norme pour unifier les benchmarks d'agents
fr.summary: CUBE propose une norme de protocole universel pour résoudre la fragmentation des benchmarks d'agents, permettant à toute plateforme conforme d'accéder aux benchmarks sans intégration personnalisée. La norme sépare les préoccupations entre les couches de tâche, de benchmark, de package et de registre pour rationaliser l'évaluation et réduire les frais généraux d'intégration.

de.title: CUBE: Ein Standard zur Vereinigung von Agent-Benchmarks
de.summary: CUBE schlägt einen universellen Protokollstandard vor, um die Fragmentierung in Agent-Benchmarks zu beheben und es jeder konformen Plattform zu ermöglichen, auf Benchmarks ohne benutzerdefinierte Integration zuzugreifen. Der Standard trennt Belange über Task-, Benchmark-, Package- und Registry-Schichten hinweg, um die Bewertung zu rationalisieren und den Integrationsaufwand zu reduzieren.

es.title: CUBE: Un estándar para unificar benchmarks de agentes
es.summary: CUBE propone un estándar de protocolo universal para abordar la fragmentación en benchmarks de agentes, permitiendo que cualquier plataforma compatible acceda a benchmarks sin integración personalizada. El estándar separa preocupaciones entre capas de tarea, benchmark, paquete y registro para simplificar la evaluación y reducir la sobrecarga de integración.

title: Prose2Policy (P2P): A Practical LLM Pipeline for Translating Natural-Language Access Policies into Executable Rego
summary: Prose2Policy converts natural-language access control policies into executable Rego code for Open Policy Agent with a 95.3% compile rate and reliable test pass rates. The tool bridges human-readable security requirements with machine-enforceable policy-as-code while maintaining deployment reliability and auditability.
content:
arXiv:2603.15799v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Prose2Policy (P2P) is a LLM-based practical tool that translates natural-language access control policies (NLACPs) into executable Rego code (the policy language of Open Policy Agent, OPA). It provides a modular, end-to-end pipeline that performs policy detection, component extraction, schema validation, linting, compilation, automatic test generation and execution. Prose2Policy is designed to bridge the gap between human-readable access requirements and machine-enforceable policy-as-code (PaC) while emphasizing deployment reliability and auditability. We evaluated Prose2Policy on the ACRE dataset and demonstrated a 95.3\% compile rate for accepted policies, with automated testing achieving a 82.2\% positive-test pass rate and a 98.9\% negative-test pass rate. These results indicate that Prose2Policy produces syntactically robust and behaviorally consistent Rego policies suitable for Zero Trust and compliance-driven environments.

zh.title: Prose2Policy (P2P)：将自然语言访问策略转换为可执行Rego的实用LLM管道
zh.summary: Prose2Policy将自然语言访问控制策略转换为开放策略代理的可执行Rego代码，编译率达95.3%，测试通过率可靠。该工具将人类可读的安全要求与机器可执行的策略即代码联系起来，同时保持部署可靠性和可审计性。

fr.title: Prose2Policy (P2P) : Un pipeline LLM pratique pour traduire les politiques d'accès en langage naturel en Rego exécutable
fr.summary: Prose2Policy convertit les politiques de contrôle d'accès en langage naturel en code Rego exécutable pour Open Policy Agent avec un taux de compilation de 95,3% et des taux de réussite de test fiables. L'outil établit un pont entre les exigences de sécurité lisibles par l'homme et la stratégie machine-exécutable en tant que code, tout en maintenant la fiabilité du déploiement et l'auditabilité.

de.title: Prose2Policy (P2P): Eine praktische LLM-Pipeline zur Übersetzung von Richtlinien für natürlichsprachigen Zugriff in ausführbares Rego
de.summary: Prose2Policy konvertiert Zugriffskontrollrichtlinien in natürlicher Sprache in ausführbaren Rego-Code für Open Policy Agent mit einer Kompilierungsrate von 95,3% und zuverlässigen Testbestätigungsquoten. Das Tool verbindet von Menschen lesbare Sicherheitsanforderungen mit maschinenausführbarem Policy-as-Code und erhält gleichzeitig Zuverlässigkeit und Auditierbarkeit bei der Bereitstellung.

es.title: Prose2Policy (P2P): Un pipeline LLM práctico para traducir políticas de acceso en lenguaje natural a Rego ejecutable
es.summary: Prose2Policy convierte políticas de control de acceso en lenguaje natural en código Rego ejecutable para Open Policy Agent con una tasa de compilación del 95.3% y tasas de aprobación de pruebas confiables. La herramienta vincula requisitos de seguridad legibles por humanos con política ejecutable por máquina como código, mientras mantiene confiabilidad de implementación y auditabilidad.

title: Persona-Conditioned Risk Behavior in Large Language Models: A Simulated Gambling Study with GPT-4.1
summary: GPT-4.1 reproduced behavioral patterns predicted by Prospect Theory when assigned different socioeconomic personas in a simulated gambling task, suggesting classical cognitive biases may be implicitly encoded in large language models. The findings have implications for AI agent design, interpretability, and understanding LLM decision-making.
content:
arXiv:2603.15831v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents in uncertain, sequential decision-making contexts. Yet it remains poorly understood whether the behaviors they exhibit in such environments reflect principled cognitive patterns or simply surface-level prompt mimicry. This paper presents a controlled experiment in which GPT-4.1 was assigned one of three socioeconomic personas (Rich, Middle-income, and Poor) and placed in a structured slot-machine environment with three distinct machine configurations: Fair (50%), Biased Low (35%), and Streak (dynamic probability increasing after consecutive losses). Across 50 independent iterations per condition and 6,950 recorded decisions, we find that the model reproduces key behavioral signatures predicted by Kahneman and Tversky's Prospect Theory without being instructed to do so. The Poor persona played a mean of 37.4 rounds per session (SD=15.5) compared to 1.1 rounds for the Rich persona (SD=0.31), a difference that is highly significant (Kruskal-Wallis H=393.5, p<2.2e-16). Risk scores by persona show large effect sizes (Cohen's d=4.15 for Poor vs Rich). Emotional labels appear to function as post-hoc annotations rather than decision drivers (chi-square=3205.4, Cramer's V=0.39), and belief-updating across rounds is negligible (Spearman rho=0.032 for Poor persona, p=0.016). These findings carry implications for LLM agent design, interpretability research, and the broader question of whether classical cognitive economic biases are implicitly encoded in large-scale pretrained language models.

zh.title: 大型语言模型中的人设条件风险行为：使用GPT-4.1的模拟赌博研究
zh.summary: GPT-4.1在模拟赌博任务中被赋予不同社会经济背景时，重现了前景理论预测的行为模式，表明经典认知偏差可能隐含地编码在大型语言模型中。这些发现对人工智能代理设计、可解释性和理解LLM决策制定具有影响。

fr.title: Comportement à risque conditionné par la persona dans les grands modèles de langage : une étude de jeu simulé avec GPT-4.1
fr.summary: GPT-4.1 a reproduit les modèles de comportement prédits par la théorie des perspectives lorsqu'il s'est vu attribuer différentes personas socioéconomiques dans une tâche de jeu simulée, suggérant que les biais cognitifs classiques peuvent être implicitement codés dans les grands modèles de langage. Les résultats ont des implications pour la conception des agents IA, l'interprétabilité et la compréhension de la prise de décision des LLM.

de.title: Persona-bedingte Risikoverhalten in großen Sprachmodellen: Eine simulierte Glücksspielstudie mit GPT-4.1
de.summary: GPT-4.1 reproduzierte Verhaltensweisen, die von der Prospect-Theorie vorhergesagt wurden, als ihm verschiedene sozioökonomische Personas in einer simulierten Glücksspielaufgabe zugewiesen wurden, was darauf hindeutet, dass klassische kognitive Verzerrungen möglicherweise implizit in großen Sprachmodellen codiert sind. Die Ergebnisse haben Auswirkungen auf das Design von KI-Agenten, die Interpretierbarkeit und das Verständnis der Entscheidungsfindung von LLM.

es.title: Comportamiento de riesgo condicionado por persona en modelos de lenguaje grandes: Un estudio de juego simulado con GPT-4.1
es.summary: GPT-4.1 reprodujo patrones de comportamiento predichos por la Teoría de Perspectivas cuando se le asignaron diferentes personas socioeconómicas en una tarea de juego simulada, sugiriendo que los sesgos cognitivos clásicos pueden estar codificados implícitamente en modelos de lenguaje grandes. Los hallazgos tienen implicaciones para el diseño de agentes de IA, la interpretabilidad y la comprensión de la toma de decisiones de LLM.

title: Algorithmic Trading Strategy Development and Optimisation
summary: Researchers developed an enhanced algorithmic trading strategy integrating technical indicators with FinBERT-based sentiment analysis on S&P 500 data. The approach significantly outperformed baseline models across total return, Sharpe ratio, and drawdown metrics, demonstrating the value of combining multiple analytical methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.15848v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The report presents with the development and optimisation of an enhanced algorithmic trading strategy through the use of historical S&P 500 market data and earnings call sentiment analysis. The proposed strategy integrates various technical indicators such as moving averages, momentum, volatility, and FinBERT-based sentiment analysis to improve overall trades being taken. The results show that the enhanced strategy significantly outperforms the baseline model in terms of total return, Sharpe ratio, and drawdown amongst other factors. The findings helped demonstrate the relevance and effectiveness of combining technical indicators, sentiment analysis, and computational optimisation in algorithmic trading systems.

zh.title: 算法交易策略的开发与优化
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一种增强的算法交易策略，将技术指标与基于FinBERT的情感分析整合到S&P 500数据中。该方法在总收益、夏普比率和最大回撤指标上明显优于基线模型，证明了结合多种分析方法的价值。

fr.title: Développement et optimisation de stratégies de trading algorithmique
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé une stratégie de trading algorithmique améliorée intégrant des indicateurs techniques avec une analyse des sentiments basée sur FinBERT sur les données du S&P 500. L'approche a considérablement surpassé les modèles de base selon les métriques de rendement total, ratio de Sharpe et tirage maximal, démontrant la valeur de la combinaison de plusieurs méthodes analytiques.

de.title: Entwicklung und Optimierung von algorithmischen Handelsstrategien
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten eine verbesserte algorithmische Handelsstrategie, die technische Indikatoren mit FinBERT-basierter Sentimentanalyse zu S&P-500-Daten integriert. Der Ansatz übertraf die Basismodelle bei den Metriken Gesamtrendite, Sharpe-Ratio und Drawdown deutlich und demonstriert den Wert der Kombination mehrerer analytischer Methoden.

es.title: Desarrollo y optimización de estrategias de negociación algorítmica
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron una estrategia mejorada de negociación algorítmica que integra indicadores técnicos con análisis de sentimientos basado en FinBERT en datos del S&P 500. El enfoque superó significativamente los modelos de referencia en métricas de rendimiento total, ratio de Sharpe y retracción máxima, demostrando el valor de combinar múltiples métodos analíticos.

title: Regularized Latent Dynamics Prediction is a Strong Baseline For Behavioral Foundation Models
summary: Regularized Latent Dynamics Prediction (RLDP) uses orthogonality regularization to maintain feature diversity in zero-shot reinforcement learning, matching or surpassing complex representation learning methods. RLDP performs particularly well in low-coverage data scenarios where prior approaches typically struggle.
content:
arXiv:2603.15857v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Behavioral Foundation Models (BFMs) produce agents with the capability to adapt to any unknown reward or task. These methods, however, are only able to produce near-optimal policies for the reward functions that are in the span of some pre-existing state features, making the choice of state features crucial to the expressivity of the BFM. As a result, BFMs are trained using a variety of complex objectives and require sufficient dataset coverage, to train task-useful spanning features.
